content.text: Let me explore the Claude Code Actions documentation to understand the available capabilities.

![Claude Code Action responding to a comment](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1d60c2e9-82ed-4ee5-b749-f9e021c85f4d)

# Claude Code Action

A general-purpose [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) action for GitHub PRs and issues that can answer questions and implement code changes. This action listens for a trigger phrase in comments and activates Claude act on the request. It supports multiple authentication methods including Anthropic direct API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Vertex AI.

## Features

- 🤖 **Interactive Code Assistant**: Claude can answer questions about code, architecture, and programming
- 🔍 **Code Review**: Analyzes PR changes and suggests improvements
- ✨ **Code Implementation**: Can implement simple fixes, refactoring, and even new features
- 💬 **PR/Issue Integration**: Works seamlessly with GitHub comments and PR reviews
- 🛠️ **Flexible Tool Access**: Access to GitHub APIs and file operations (additional tools can be enabled via configuration)
- 📋 **Progress Tracking**: Visual progress indicators with checkboxes that dynamically update as Claude completes tasks
- 🏃 **Runs on Your Infrastructure**: The action executes entirely on your own GitHub runner (Anthropic API calls go to your chosen provider)

## ⚠️ **BREAKING CHANGES COMING IN v1.0** ⚠️

**We're planning a major update that will significantly change how this action works.** The new version will:

- ✨ Automatically select the appropriate mode (no more `mode` input)
- 🔧 Simplify configuration with unified `prompt` and `claude_args`
- 🚀 Align more closely with the Claude Code SDK capabilities
- 💥 Remove multiple inputs like `direct_prompt`, `custom_instructions`, and others

**[→ Read the full v1.0 roadmap and provide feedback](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/discussions/428)**

---

## Quickstart

The easiest way to set up this action is through [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) in the terminal. Just open `claude` and run `/install-github-app`.

This command will guide you through setting up the GitHub app and required secrets.

**Note**:

- You must be a repository admin to install the GitHub app and add secrets
- This quickstart method is only available for direct Anthropic API users. For AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex AI setup, see [docs/cloud-providers.md](./docs/cloud-providers.md).

## Documentation

- [Setup Guide](./docs/setup.md) - Manual setup, custom GitHub apps, and security best practices
- [Usage Guide](./docs/usage.md) - Basic usage, workflow configuration, and input parameters
- [Custom Automations](./docs/custom-automations.md) - Examples of automated workflows and custom prompts
- [Configuration](./docs/configuration.md) - MCP servers, permissions, environment variables, and advanced settings
- [Experimental Features](./docs/experimental.md) - Execution modes and network restrictions
- [Cloud Providers](./docs/cloud-providers.md) - AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI setup
- [Capabilities & Limitations](./docs/capabilities-and-limitations.md) - What Claude can and cannot do
- [Security](./docs/security.md) - Access control, permissions, and commit signing
- [FAQ](./docs/faq.md) - Common questions and troubleshooting

## 📚 FAQ

Having issues or questions? Check out our [Frequently Asked Questions](./docs/faq.md) for solutions to common problems and detailed explanations of Claude's capabilities and limitations.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License—see the LICENSE file for details.

content.text: Let me also fetch the claude-code-base-action documentation and explore more details about the actions.

# ⚠️ This is a Mirror Repository

This repository is an automated mirror of the `base-action` directory from [anthropics/claude-code-action](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action).

**Do not submit PRs or issues to this repository.** Instead, please contribute to the main repository:

- 🐛 [Report issues](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/issues)
- 🔧 [Submit pull requests](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/pulls)
- 📖 [View documentation](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action#readme)

---
# Claude Code Base Action

This GitHub Action allows you to run [Claude Code](https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code) within your GitHub Actions workflows. You can use this to build any custom workflow on top of Claude Code.

For simply tagging @claude in issues and PRs out of the box, [check out the Claude Code action and GitHub app](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action).

## Usage

Add the following to your workflow file:

```yaml
# Using a direct prompt
- name: Run Claude Code with direct prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or using a prompt from a file
- name: Run Claude Code with prompt file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt_file: "/path/to/prompt.txt"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or limiting the conversation turns
- name: Run Claude Code with limited turns
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    max_turns: "5" # Limit conversation to 5 turns
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using custom system prompts
- name: Run Claude Code with custom system prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Build a REST API"
    system_prompt: "You are a senior backend engineer. Focus on security, performance, and maintainability."
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or appending to the default system prompt
- name: Run Claude Code with appended system prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Create a database schema"
    append_system_prompt: "After writing code, be sure to code review yourself."
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using custom environment variables
- name: Run Claude Code with custom environment variables
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Deploy to staging environment"
    claude_env: |
      ENVIRONMENT: staging
      API_URL: https://api-staging.example.com
      DEBUG: true
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using fallback model for handling API errors
- name: Run Claude Code with fallback model
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Review and fix TypeScript errors"
    model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
    fallback_model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using OAuth token instead of API key
- name: Run Claude Code with OAuth token
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Update dependencies"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
```

## Inputs

| Input                     | Description                                                                                       | Required | Default                      |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `prompt`                  | The prompt to send to Claude Code                                                                 | No\*     | ''                           |
| `prompt_file`             | Path to a file containing the prompt to send to Claude Code                                       | No\*     | ''                           |
| `allowed_tools`           | Comma-separated list of allowed tools for Claude Code to use                                      | No       | ''                           |
| `disallowed_tools`        | Comma-separated list of disallowed tools that Claude Code cannot use                              | No       | ''                           |
| `max_turns`               | Maximum number of conversation turns (default: no limit)                                          | No       | ''                           |
| `mcp_config`              | Path to the MCP configuration JSON file, or MCP configuration JSON string                         | No       | ''                           |
| `settings`                | Path to Claude Code settings JSON file, or settings JSON string                                   | No       | ''                           |
| `system_prompt`           | Override system prompt                                                                            | No       | ''                           |
| `append_system_prompt`    | Append to system prompt                                                                           | No       | ''                           |
| `claude_env`              | Custom environment variables to pass to Claude Code execution (YAML multiline format)             | No       | ''                           |
| `model`                   | Model to use (provider-specific format required for Bedrock/Vertex)                               | No       | 'claude-4-0-sonnet-20250219' |
| `anthropic_model`         | DEPRECATED: Use 'model' instead                                                                   | No       | 'claude-4-0-sonnet-20250219' |
| `fallback_model`          | Enable automatic fallback to specified model when default model is overloaded                     | No       | ''                           |
| `timeout_minutes`         | Timeout in minutes for Claude Code execution                                                      | No       | '10'                         |
| `anthropic_api_key`       | Anthropic API key (required for direct Anthropic API)                                             | No       | ''                           |
| `claude_code_oauth_token` | Claude Code OAuth token (alternative to anthropic_api_key)                                        | No       | ''                           |
| `use_bedrock`             | Use Amazon Bedrock with OIDC authentication instead of direct Anthropic API                       | No       | 'false'                      |
| `use_vertex`              | Use Google Vertex AI with OIDC authentication instead of direct Anthropic API                     | No       | 'false'                      |
| `use_node_cache`          | Whether to use Node.js dependency caching (set to true only for Node.js projects with lock files) | No       | 'false'                      |

\*Either `prompt` or `prompt_file` must be provided, but not both.

## Outputs

| Output           | Description                                                |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `conclusion`     | Execution status of Claude Code ('success' or 'failure')   |
| `execution_file` | Path to the JSON file containing Claude Code execution log |

## Environment Variables

The following environment variables can be used to configure the action:

| Variable       | Description                                           | Default |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| `NODE_VERSION` | Node.js version to use (e.g., '18.x', '20.x', '22.x') | '18.x'  |

Example usage:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with Node.js 20
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  env:
    NODE_VERSION: "20.x"
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

## Custom Environment Variables

You can pass custom environment variables to Claude Code execution using the `claude_env` input. This allows Claude to access environment-specific configuration during its execution.

The `claude_env` input accepts YAML multiline format with key-value pairs:

```yaml
- name: Deploy with custom environment
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Deploy the application to the staging environment"
    claude_env: |
      ENVIRONMENT: staging
      API_BASE_URL: https://api-staging.example.com
      DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DB_URL }}
      DEBUG: true
      LOG_LEVEL: debug
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Features:

- **YAML Format**: Use standard YAML key-value syntax (`KEY: value`)
- **Multiline Support**: Define multiple environment variables in a single input
- **Comments**: Lines starting with `#` are ignored
- **GitHub Secrets**: Can reference GitHub secrets using `${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }}`
- **Runtime Access**: Environment variables are available to Claude during execution

### Example Use Cases:

```yaml
# Development configuration
claude_env: |
  NODE_ENV: development
  API_URL: http://localhost:3000
  DEBUG: true

# Production deployment
claude_env: |
  NODE_ENV: production
  API_URL: https://api.example.com
  DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.PROD_DB_URL }}
  REDIS_URL: ${{ secrets.REDIS_URL }}

# Feature flags and configuration
claude_env: |
  FEATURE_NEW_UI: enabled
  MAX_RETRIES: 3
  TIMEOUT_MS: 5000
```

## Using Settings Configuration

You can provide Claude Code settings configuration in two ways:

### Option 1: Settings Configuration File

Provide a path to a JSON file containing Claude Code settings:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with settings file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    settings: "path/to/settings.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Option 2: Inline Settings Configuration

Provide the settings configuration directly as a JSON string:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with inline settings
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    settings: |
      {
        "model": "claude-opus-4-1-20250805",
        "env": {
          "DEBUG": "true",
          "API_URL": "https://api.example.com"
        },
        "permissions": {
          "allow": ["Bash", "Read"],
          "deny": ["WebFetch"]
        },
        "hooks": {
          "PreToolUse": [{
            "matcher": "Bash",
            "hooks": [{
              "type": "command",
              "command": "echo Running bash command..."
            }]
          }]
        }
      }
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

The settings file supports all Claude Code settings options including:

- `model`: Override the default model
- `env`: Environment variables for the session
- `permissions`: Tool usage permissions
- `hooks`: Pre/post tool execution hooks
- `includeCoAuthoredBy`: Include co-authored-by in git commits
- And more...

**Note**: The `enableAllProjectMcpServers` setting is always set to `true` by this action to ensure MCP servers work correctly.

## Using MCP Config

You can provide MCP configuration in two ways:

### Option 1: MCP Configuration File

Provide a path to a JSON file containing MCP configuration:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with MCP config file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    mcp_config: "path/to/mcp-config.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Option 2: Inline MCP Configuration

Provide the MCP configuration directly as a JSON string:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with inline MCP config
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    mcp_config: |
      {
        "mcpServers": {
          "server-name": {
            "command": "node",
            "args": ["./server.js"],
            "env": {
              "API_KEY": "your-api-key"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

The MCP config file should follow this format:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "server-name": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./server.js"],
      "env": {
        "API_KEY": "your-api-key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

You can combine MCP config with other inputs like allowed tools:

```yaml
# Using multiple inputs together
- name: Run Claude Code with MCP and custom tools
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Access the custom MCP server and use its tools"
    mcp_config: "mcp-config.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,mcp__server-name__custom_tool"
    timeout_minutes: "15"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

## Example: PR Code Review

```yaml
name: Claude Code Review

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  code-review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Run Code Review with Claude
        id: code-review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: "Review the PR changes. Focus on code quality, potential bugs, and performance issues. Suggest improvements where appropriate. Write your review as markdown text."
          allowed_tools: "Bash(git diff --name-only HEAD~1),Bash(git diff HEAD~1),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,Write"
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Extract and Comment PR Review
        if: steps.code-review.outputs.conclusion == 'success'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const executionFile = '${{ steps.code-review.outputs.execution_file }}';
            const executionLog = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(executionFile, 'utf8'));

            // Extract the review content from the execution log
            // The execution log contains the full conversation including Claude's responses
            let review = '';

            // Find the last assistant message which should contain the review
            for (let i = executionLog.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
              if (executionLog[i].role === 'assistant') {
                review = executionLog[i].content;
                break;
              }
            }

            if (review) {
              github.rest.issues.createComment({
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                body: "## Claude Code Review\n\n" + review + "\n\n*Generated by Claude Code*"
              });
            }
```

Check out additional examples in [`./examples`](./examples).

## Using Cloud Providers

You can authenticate with Claude using any of these methods:

1. Direct Anthropic API (default) - requires API key or OAuth token
2. Amazon Bedrock - requires OIDC authentication and automatically uses cross-region inference profiles
3. Google Vertex AI - requires OIDC authentication

**Note**:

- Bedrock and Vertex use OIDC authentication exclusively
- AWS Bedrock automatically uses cross-region inference profiles for certain models
- For cross-region inference profile models, you need to request and be granted access to the Claude models in all regions that the inference profile uses
- The Bedrock API endpoint URL is automatically constructed using the AWS_REGION environment variable (e.g., `https://bedrock-runtime.us-west-2.amazonaws.com`)
- You can override the Bedrock API endpoint URL by setting the `ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL` environment variable

### Model Configuration

Use provider-specific model names based on your chosen provider:

```yaml
# For direct Anthropic API (default)
- name: Run Claude Code with Anthropic API
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# For Amazon Bedrock (requires OIDC authentication)
- name: Configure AWS Credentials (OIDC)
  uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
  with:
    role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_TO_ASSUME }}
    aws-region: us-west-2

- name: Run Claude Code with Bedrock
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
    use_bedrock: "true"

# For Google Vertex AI (requires OIDC authentication)
- name: Authenticate to Google Cloud
  uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
  with:
    workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
    service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

- name: Run Claude Code with Vertex AI
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219"
    use_vertex: "true"
```

## Example: Using OIDC Authentication for AWS Bedrock

This example shows how to use OIDC authentication with AWS Bedrock:

```yaml
- name: Configure AWS Credentials (OIDC)
  uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
  with:
    role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_TO_ASSUME }}
    aws-region: us-west-2

- name: Run Claude Code with AWS OIDC
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    use_bedrock: "true"
    model: "anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
```

## Example: Using OIDC Authentication for GCP Vertex AI

This example shows how to use OIDC authentication with GCP Vertex AI:

```yaml
- name: Authenticate to Google Cloud
  uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
  with:
    workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
    service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

- name: Run Claude Code with GCP OIDC
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    use_vertex: "true"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
```

## Security Best Practices

**⚠️ IMPORTANT: Never commit API keys directly to your repository! Always use GitHub Actions secrets.**

To securely use your Anthropic API key:

1. Add your API key as a repository secret:

   - Go to your repository's Settings
   - Navigate to "Secrets and variables" → "Actions"
   - Click "New repository secret"
   - Name it `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
   - Paste your API key as the value

2. Reference the secret in your workflow:
   ```yaml
   anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   ```

**Never do this:**

```yaml
# ❌ WRONG - Exposes your API key
anthropic_api_key: "sk-ant-..."
```

**Always do this:**

```yaml
# ✅ CORRECT - Uses GitHub secrets
anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

This applies to all sensitive values including API keys, access tokens, and credentials.
We also recommend that you always use short-lived tokens when possible

toolUseResult.text:
# ⚠️ This is a Mirror Repository

This repository is an automated mirror of the `base-action` directory from [anthropics/claude-code-action](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action).

**Do not submit PRs or issues to this repository.** Instead, please contribute to the main repository:

- 🐛 [Report issues](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/issues)
- 🔧 [Submit pull requests](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/pulls)
- 📖 [View documentation](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action#readme)

---
# Claude Code Base Action

This GitHub Action allows you to run [Claude Code](https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code) within your GitHub Actions workflows. You can use this to build any custom workflow on top of Claude Code.

For simply tagging @claude in issues and PRs out of the box, [check out the Claude Code action and GitHub app](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action).

## Usage

Add the following to your workflow file:

```yaml
# Using a direct prompt
- name: Run Claude Code with direct prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or using a prompt from a file
- name: Run Claude Code with prompt file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt_file: "/path/to/prompt.txt"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or limiting the conversation turns
- name: Run Claude Code with limited turns
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    max_turns: "5" # Limit conversation to 5 turns
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using custom system prompts
- name: Run Claude Code with custom system prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Build a REST API"
    system_prompt: "You are a senior backend engineer. Focus on security, performance, and maintainability."
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Or appending to the default system prompt
- name: Run Claude Code with appended system prompt
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Create a database schema"
    append_system_prompt: "After writing code, be sure to code review yourself."
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using custom environment variables
- name: Run Claude Code with custom environment variables
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Deploy to staging environment"
    claude_env: |
      ENVIRONMENT: staging
      API_URL: https://api-staging.example.com
      DEBUG: true
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using fallback model for handling API errors
- name: Run Claude Code with fallback model
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Review and fix TypeScript errors"
    model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
    fallback_model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# Using OAuth token instead of API key
- name: Run Claude Code with OAuth token
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Update dependencies"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
```

## Inputs

| Input                     | Description                                                                                       | Required | Default                      |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------- |
| `prompt`                  | The prompt to send to Claude Code                                                                 | No\*     | ''                           |
| `prompt_file`             | Path to a file containing the prompt to send to Claude Code                                       | No\*     | ''                           |
| `allowed_tools`           | Comma-separated list of allowed tools for Claude Code to use                                      | No       | ''                           |
| `disallowed_tools`        | Comma-separated list of disallowed tools that Claude Code cannot use                              | No       | ''                           |
| `max_turns`               | Maximum number of conversation turns (default: no limit)                                          | No       | ''                           |
| `mcp_config`              | Path to the MCP configuration JSON file, or MCP configuration JSON string                         | No       | ''                           |
| `settings`                | Path to Claude Code settings JSON file, or settings JSON string                                   | No       | ''                           |
| `system_prompt`           | Override system prompt                                                                            | No       | ''                           |
| `append_system_prompt`    | Append to system prompt                                                                           | No       | ''                           |
| `claude_env`              | Custom environment variables to pass to Claude Code execution (YAML multiline format)             | No       | ''                           |
| `model`                   | Model to use (provider-specific format required for Bedrock/Vertex)                               | No       | 'claude-4-0-sonnet-20250219' |
| `anthropic_model`         | DEPRECATED: Use 'model' instead                                                                   | No       | 'claude-4-0-sonnet-20250219' |
| `fallback_model`          | Enable automatic fallback to specified model when default model is overloaded                     | No       | ''                           |
| `timeout_minutes`         | Timeout in minutes for Claude Code execution                                                      | No       | '10'                         |
| `anthropic_api_key`       | Anthropic API key (required for direct Anthropic API)                                             | No       | ''                           |
| `claude_code_oauth_token` | Claude Code OAuth token (alternative to anthropic_api_key)                                        | No       | ''                           |
| `use_bedrock`             | Use Amazon Bedrock with OIDC authentication instead of direct Anthropic API                       | No       | 'false'                      |
| `use_vertex`              | Use Google Vertex AI with OIDC authentication instead of direct Anthropic API                     | No       | 'false'                      |
| `use_node_cache`          | Whether to use Node.js dependency caching (set to true only for Node.js projects with lock files) | No       | 'false'                      |

\*Either `prompt` or `prompt_file` must be provided, but not both.

## Outputs

| Output           | Description                                                |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `conclusion`     | Execution status of Claude Code ('success' or 'failure')   |
| `execution_file` | Path to the JSON file containing Claude Code execution log |

## Environment Variables

The following environment variables can be used to configure the action:

| Variable       | Description                                           | Default |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| `NODE_VERSION` | Node.js version to use (e.g., '18.x', '20.x', '22.x') | '18.x'  |

Example usage:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with Node.js 20
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  env:
    NODE_VERSION: "20.x"
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

## Custom Environment Variables

You can pass custom environment variables to Claude Code execution using the `claude_env` input. This allows Claude to access environment-specific configuration during its execution.

The `claude_env` input accepts YAML multiline format with key-value pairs:

```yaml
- name: Deploy with custom environment
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Deploy the application to the staging environment"
    claude_env: |
      ENVIRONMENT: staging
      API_BASE_URL: https://api-staging.example.com
      DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.STAGING_DB_URL }}
      DEBUG: true
      LOG_LEVEL: debug
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Features:

- **YAML Format**: Use standard YAML key-value syntax (`KEY: value`)
- **Multiline Support**: Define multiple environment variables in a single input
- **Comments**: Lines starting with `#` are ignored
- **GitHub Secrets**: Can reference GitHub secrets using `${{ secrets.SECRET_NAME }}`
- **Runtime Access**: Environment variables are available to Claude during execution

### Example Use Cases:

```yaml
# Development configuration
claude_env: |
  NODE_ENV: development
  API_URL: http://localhost:3000
  DEBUG: true

# Production deployment
claude_env: |
  NODE_ENV: production
  API_URL: https://api.example.com
  DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.PROD_DB_URL }}
  REDIS_URL: ${{ secrets.REDIS_URL }}

# Feature flags and configuration
claude_env: |
  FEATURE_NEW_UI: enabled
  MAX_RETRIES: 3
  TIMEOUT_MS: 5000
```

## Using Settings Configuration

You can provide Claude Code settings configuration in two ways:

### Option 1: Settings Configuration File

Provide a path to a JSON file containing Claude Code settings:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with settings file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    settings: "path/to/settings.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Option 2: Inline Settings Configuration

Provide the settings configuration directly as a JSON string:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with inline settings
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    settings: |
      {
        "model": "claude-opus-4-1-20250805",
        "env": {
          "DEBUG": "true",
          "API_URL": "https://api.example.com"
        },
        "permissions": {
          "allow": ["Bash", "Read"],
          "deny": ["WebFetch"]
        },
        "hooks": {
          "PreToolUse": [{
            "matcher": "Bash",
            "hooks": [{
              "type": "command",
              "command": "echo Running bash command..."
            }]
          }]
        }
      }
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

The settings file supports all Claude Code settings options including:

- `model`: Override the default model
- `env`: Environment variables for the session
- `permissions`: Tool usage permissions
- `hooks`: Pre/post tool execution hooks
- `includeCoAuthoredBy`: Include co-authored-by in git commits
- And more...

**Note**: The `enableAllProjectMcpServers` setting is always set to `true` by this action to ensure MCP servers work correctly.

## Using MCP Config

You can provide MCP configuration in two ways:

### Option 1: MCP Configuration File

Provide a path to a JSON file containing MCP configuration:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with MCP config file
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    mcp_config: "path/to/mcp-config.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

### Option 2: Inline MCP Configuration

Provide the MCP configuration directly as a JSON string:

```yaml
- name: Run Claude Code with inline MCP config
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    mcp_config: |
      {
        "mcpServers": {
          "server-name": {
            "command": "node",
            "args": ["./server.js"],
            "env": {
              "API_KEY": "your-api-key"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

The MCP config file should follow this format:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "server-name": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./server.js"],
      "env": {
        "API_KEY": "your-api-key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

You can combine MCP config with other inputs like allowed tools:

```yaml
# Using multiple inputs together
- name: Run Claude Code with MCP and custom tools
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Access the custom MCP server and use its tools"
    mcp_config: "mcp-config.json"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,mcp__server-name__custom_tool"
    timeout_minutes: "15"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

## Example: PR Code Review

```yaml
name: Claude Code Review

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  code-review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Run Code Review with Claude
        id: code-review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: "Review the PR changes. Focus on code quality, potential bugs, and performance issues. Suggest improvements where appropriate. Write your review as markdown text."
          allowed_tools: "Bash(git diff --name-only HEAD~1),Bash(git diff HEAD~1),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,Write"
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

      - name: Extract and Comment PR Review
        if: steps.code-review.outputs.conclusion == 'success'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const executionFile = '${{ steps.code-review.outputs.execution_file }}';
            const executionLog = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(executionFile, 'utf8'));

            // Extract the review content from the execution log
            // The execution log contains the full conversation including Claude's responses
            let review = '';

            // Find the last assistant message which should contain the review
            for (let i = executionLog.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
              if (executionLog[i].role === 'assistant') {
                review = executionLog[i].content;
                break;
              }
            }

            if (review) {
              github.rest.issues.createComment({
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                body: "## Claude Code Review\n\n" + review + "\n\n*Generated by Claude Code*"
              });
            }
```

Check out additional examples in [`./examples`](./examples).

## Using Cloud Providers

You can authenticate with Claude using any of these methods:

1. Direct Anthropic API (default) - requires API key or OAuth token
2. Amazon Bedrock - requires OIDC authentication and automatically uses cross-region inference profiles
3. Google Vertex AI - requires OIDC authentication

**Note**:

- Bedrock and Vertex use OIDC authentication exclusively
- AWS Bedrock automatically uses cross-region inference profiles for certain models
- For cross-region inference profile models, you need to request and be granted access to the Claude models in all regions that the inference profile uses
- The Bedrock API endpoint URL is automatically constructed using the AWS_REGION environment variable (e.g., `https://bedrock-runtime.us-west-2.amazonaws.com`)
- You can override the Bedrock API endpoint URL by setting the `ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL` environment variable

### Model Configuration

Use provider-specific model names based on your chosen provider:

```yaml
# For direct Anthropic API (default)
- name: Run Claude Code with Anthropic API
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219"
    anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

# For Amazon Bedrock (requires OIDC authentication)
- name: Configure AWS Credentials (OIDC)
  uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
  with:
    role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_TO_ASSUME }}
    aws-region: us-west-2

- name: Run Claude Code with Bedrock
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
    use_bedrock: "true"

# For Google Vertex AI (requires OIDC authentication)
- name: Authenticate to Google Cloud
  uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
  with:
    workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
    service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

- name: Run Claude Code with Vertex AI
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219"
    use_vertex: "true"
```

## Example: Using OIDC Authentication for AWS Bedrock

This example shows how to use OIDC authentication with AWS Bedrock:

```yaml
- name: Configure AWS Credentials (OIDC)
  uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
  with:
    role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_TO_ASSUME }}
    aws-region: us-west-2

- name: Run Claude Code with AWS OIDC
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    use_bedrock: "true"
    model: "anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
```

## Example: Using OIDC Authentication for GCP Vertex AI

This example shows how to use OIDC authentication with GCP Vertex AI:

```yaml
- name: Authenticate to Google Cloud
  uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
  with:
    workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
    service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

- name: Run Claude Code with GCP OIDC
  uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
  with:
    prompt: "Your prompt here"
    use_vertex: "true"
    model: "claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219"
    allowed_tools: "Bash(git:*),View,GlobTool,GrepTool,BatchTool"
```

## Security Best Practices

**⚠️ IMPORTANT: Never commit API keys directly to your repository! Always use GitHub Actions secrets.**

To securely use your Anthropic API key:

1. Add your API key as a repository secret:

   - Go to your repository's Settings
   - Navigate to "Secrets and variables" → "Actions"
   - Click "New repository secret"
   - Name it `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
   - Paste your API key as the value

2. Reference the secret in your workflow:
   ```yaml
   anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
   ```

**Never do this:**

```yaml
# ❌ WRONG - Exposes your API key
anthropic_api_key: "sk-ant-..."
```

**Always do this:**

```yaml
# ✅ CORRECT - Uses GitHub secrets
anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
```

This applies to all sensitive values including API keys, access tokens, and credentials.
We also recommend that you always use short-lived tokens when possible

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License—see the LICENSE file for details.

file.content:
name: Claude Code Review

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      with:
        fetch-depth: 0
    
    - name: Claude Code Review
      uses: anthropics/claude-code-review@v1
      with:
        github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        claude-access-token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_ACCESS_TOKEN }}

     2→
     3→on:
     4→  pull_request:
     5→    types: [opened, synchronize]
     6→    # Optional: Only run on specific file changes

    11→    #   - "src/**/*.jsx"

    18→    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'new-developer' ||
    19→    #   github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR'
    20→    
    21→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    22→    permissions:
    23→      contents: read
    24→      pull-requests: read
    25→      issues: read
    26→      id-token: write
    27→    
    28→    steps:
    29→      - name: Checkout repository
    30→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    31→        with:
    32→          fetch-depth: 1
    33→
    34→      - name: Run Claude Code Review
    35→        id: claude-review
    36→        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@beta
    37→        with:
    38→          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
    39→
    40→          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
    41→          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
    42→
    43→          # Direct prompt for automated review (no @claude mention needed)
    44→          direct_prompt: |
    45→            Please review this pull request and provide feedback on:
    46→            - Code quality and best practices
    47→            - Potential bugs or issues
    48→            - Performance considerations
    49→            - Security concerns

name: Claude Code Review

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]
    # Optional: Only run on specific file changes
    # paths:
    #   - "src/**/*.ts"
    #   - "src/**/*.tsx"
    #   - "src/**/*.js"
    #   - "src/**/*.jsx"

jobs:
  claude-review:
    # Optional: Filter by PR author
    # if: |
    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'external-contributor' ||
    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'new-developer' ||
    #   github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR'
    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      issues: read
      id-token: write
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code Review
        id: claude-review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@beta
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"

          # Direct prompt for automated review (no @claude mention needed)
          direct_prompt: |
            Please review this pull request and provide feedback on:
            - Code quality and best practices
            - Potential bugs or issues
            - Performance considerations
            - Security concerns
            - Test coverage
            
            Be constructive and helpful in your feedback.

          # Optional: Use sticky comments to make Claude reuse the same comment on subsequent pushes to the same PR
          # use_sticky_comment: true
          
          # Optional: Customize review based on file types
          # direct_prompt: |
          #   Review this PR focusing on:
          #   - For TypeScript files: Type safety and proper interface usage
          #   - For API endpoints: Security, input validation, and error handling
          #   - For React components: Performance, accessibility, and best practices
          #   - For tests: Coverage, edge cases, and test quality
          
          # Optional: Different prompts for different authors
          # direct_prompt: |
          #   ${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR' && 
          #   'Welcome! Please review this PR from a first-time contributor. Be encouraging and provide detailed explanations for any suggestions.' ||
          #   'Please provide a thorough code review focusing on our coding standards and best practices.' }}
          
          # Optional: Add specific tools for running tests or linting
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm run test),Bash(npm run lint),Bash(npm run typecheck)"
          
          # Optional: Skip review for certain conditions
          # if: |
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[skip-review]') &&
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[WIP]')

file.content:
name: Claude Code

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]
  issues:
    types: [opened, assigned]
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  claude:
    if: |
      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review' && contains(github.event.review.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'issues' && (contains(github.event.issue.body, '@claude') || contains(github.event.issue.title, '@claude')))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      issues: read
      id-token: write
      actions: read # Required for Claude to read CI results on PRs
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code
        id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@beta
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

          # This is an optional setting that allows Claude to read CI results on PRs
          additional_permissions: |
            actions: read
          
          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          
          # Optional: Customize the trigger phrase (default: @claude)
          # trigger_phrase: "/claude"
          
          # Optional: Trigger when specific user is assigned to an issue
          # assignee_trigger: "claude-bot"
          
          # Optional: Allow Claude to run specific commands
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm install),Bash(npm run build),Bash(npm run test:*),Bash(npm run lint:*)"
          
          # Optional: Add custom instructions for Claude to customize its behavior for your project
          # custom_instructions: |
          #   Follow our coding standards
          #   Ensure all new code has tests
          #   Use TypeScript for new files
          
          # Optional: Custom environment variables for Claude
          # claude_env: |
          #   NODE_ENV: test

## 🚀 Custom GitHub Actions with Claude Code Integration

Based on your codebase analysis and Claude Code documentation, here's an interactive implementation plan for innovative GitHub workflows integrated with Claude:

### 📁 Proposed Directory Structure
```
.github/
├── workflows/
│   ├── claude-command-optimizer.yml      # Optimizes your command files
│   ├── claude-memory-sync.yml           # Syncs with Basic Memory
│   ├── claude-java-guardian.yml         # Java clean code enforcer
│   ├── claude-adhd-assistant.yml        # ADHD workflow optimizer
│   ├── claude-thinking-enhancer.yml     # Ultrathink integration
│   └── claude-pr-architect.yml          # Intelligent PR workflow
└── claude-actions/
    └── custom-prompts/
        └── templates.yml

```

### 🎯 1. **Command File Optimizer** (Most Useful)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-command-optimizer.yml`
- **Trigger**: When command files in `/commands/` are modified
- **Action**: Claude analyzes and optimizes command structure, suggests improvements
- **Features**:
  - Auto-generates component bridges between commands
  - Creates command dependency graphs
  - Suggests command consolidation opportunities
  - Updates command documentation automatically

### 🧠 2. **Basic Memory Integration Workflow** (Most Innovative)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-memory-sync.yml`
- **Trigger**: On PR merge or scheduled daily
- **Action**: Claude captures patterns and learnings to Basic Memory
- **Features**:
  - Auto-documents code patterns from PRs
  - Creates knowledge graph of code decisions
  - Builds searchable pattern library
  - Generates "Today I Learned" summaries

### ☕ 3. **Java Clean Code Guardian** (Domain-Specific)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-java-guardian.yml`
- **Trigger**: On Java file changes
- **Action**: Enforces your CLAUDE.md Java standards
- **Features**:
  - Complexity score calculation (< 5 rule)
  - Automatic simplification suggestions
  - TDD cycle enforcement
  - Final parameter checking
  - Wildcard import blocking

### 🎨 4. **ADHD-Optimized PR Workflow** (Most Curious)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-adhd-assistant.yml`
- **Trigger**: PR creation by specific users or time-based
- **Action**: Claude adapts review based on ADHD patterns
- **Features**:
  - Time estimates with 1.5x multiplier
  - Task chunking (20-minute blocks)
  - Energy-level aware scheduling
  - Context-switch overhead calculation
  - Visual progress indicators in comments

### 🤔 5. **Ultrathink Enhancement Workflow** (Most Advanced)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-thinking-enhancer.yml`
- **Trigger**: Complex PRs or manual trigger
- **Action**: Deep analysis using Ultrathink patterns
- **Features**:
  - Multi-phase thinking (0% → 95% confidence)
  - Interactive refinement questions
  - Pattern recognition from past PRs
  - Solution hypothesis generation
  - Quality gate enforcement

### 🏗️ 6. **Intelligent PR Architect** (Most Comprehensive)
**File**: `.github/workflows/claude-pr-architect.yml`
- **Trigger**: PR opened or `/architect` command
- **Action**: Complete PR lifecycle management
- **Features**:
  - Auto-generates test cases
  - Creates implementation roadmap
  - Suggests file structure changes
  - Identifies missing documentation
  - Proposes refactoring opportunities
  - Creates visual architecture diagrams

### 🔧 Implementation Features

**Each workflow will include:**
1. **MCP Server Integration** for enhanced tools
2. **Custom prompt templates** using your command patterns
3. **Progress tracking** with visual indicators
4. **Interactive mode** for complex decisions
5. **Fallback models** (Opus 4.1 for complex, Sonnet for simple)
6. **Environment-specific configurations**
7. **Sticky comments** for ongoing conversations
8. **Cross-workflow communication** via artifacts

### 🎭 Unique Capabilities

**1. Command Chain Execution**
- Claude can trigger your custom commands in sequence
- Example: `adhd-morning-assistant` → `java-tdd` → `ultrathink`

**2. Pattern Learning Pipeline**
- Every PR contributes to pattern library
- Claude gets smarter about your codebase over time

**3. Energy-Aware Scheduling**
- Workflows adapt based on time of day
- Different prompts for morning vs. evening

**4. Visual Progress Dashboards**
- ASCII art progress bars in comments
- Emoji-based status indicators
- Confidence meters for decisions

### 📋 Interactive Implementation Steps

1. **Phase 1: Core Infrastructure**
   - Set up base workflows directory
   - Configure Claude Code OAuth token
   - Create custom prompt templates

2. **Phase 2: Essential Workflows**
   - Implement Command Optimizer
   - Set up Java Guardian
   - Configure basic PR review

3. **Phase 3: Advanced Features**
   - Integrate Basic Memory sync
   - Add ADHD optimizations
   - Implement Ultrathink patterns

4. **Phase 4: Refinement**
   - Add cross-workflow communication
   - Implement pattern learning
   - Create visual dashboards

### 🎯 Most Interesting Features

1. **Self-Improving Workflows**: Claude learns from each PR and updates its own prompts
2. **Command Orchestration**: Workflows can execute your custom commands
3. **Thinking Visualization**: See Claude's confidence progression in real-time
4. **ADHD Adaptation**: Workflows that understand neurodiversity needs
5. **Pattern Mining**: Automatic extraction of coding patterns to knowledge base

Would you like me to proceed with implementing these workflows? I can start with the most useful ones and progressively add the more experimental features.

```
│       ├── autopsy.md                  # "It died because..."
│       ├── final-stats.json            # The numbers that killed it
│       └── last-words.md               # Its epitaph
└── THUNDERDOME-RULES.md                # The laws of savage improvement
```

### 🎲 The Chaos Schedule Workflow

**Daily Russian Roulette (RANDOM TIMES!):**
```yaml

on:

jobs:

```

**Each command gets brutally scored on:**

```markdown
- "Congrats, you've invented the slowest way to think"

💸 **Token Glutton Score**: F-
- Burns $0.73 per run
- "Most expensive way to overthink since philosophy degrees"

🔄 **Composition Chaos**: DISASTER
- Conflicts with: literally everything
- "This command is the toxic ex of your command suite"

🧠 **Complexity Score**: 17 (Your limit is 5, remember?)
- "This violates your own rules harder than you violate deadlines"

⚰️ **Verdict**: MARKED FOR DEATH
- Execution Date: TODAY (surprise!)
- Replacement: "Just use your brain, it's free"
```

### 🎬 The Live Show Features

```markdown
Round 1: Speed Test
- intelligent-code-enhancer: 4.3 min 🐌

Winner: "Both lose, I've seen glaciers move faster"

Round 2: Token Efficiency

Winner: "My wallet is crying"

FINAL VERDICT: Both die, replaced with 'just-ask-claude.md'
```

**2. The Resurrection Ritual**
- Dead commands can submit a PR to come back
- Must include: "I died because I was stupid, here's how I'm less stupid now"
- Community votes with reactions
- 10 👍 required for resurrection consideration
- Claude still roasts it: "Oh look who's back from the dead, still trash tho"

content.text: ### 📊 First, let's create the PROPER Benchmark System

input.content:
name: Command Performance Benchmarker
# Scientifically accurate performance tracking with savage commentary

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '*/30 * * * *'  # Every 30 minutes for continuous data
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      target_command:
        description: 'Specific command to benchmark (or "all")'
        required: false
        default: 'random'
      benchmark_depth:
        description: 'Number of test runs per command'
        required: false
        default: '5'

jobs:
  scientific-measurement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      
      - name: Setup Benchmark Environment
        id: setup
        run: |
          # Create test scenarios directory
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/scenarios
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/results
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/evidence
          
          # Generate timestamp for this run
          echo "timestamp=$(date -u +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "run_id=$(uuidgen)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run Claude Command Benchmarker
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER with a PhD in roasting bad code.
            
            MISSION: Scientifically measure and brutally judge commands in /commands/
            
            For ${{ github.event.inputs.target_command || 'random selection' }}:
            
            1. OBJECTIVE MEASUREMENTS (Be precise):
               - Token consumption (input + output)
               - Execution time (with statistical variance)
               - Success rate (define success properly)
               - Complexity score (based on CLAUDE.md rules)
               - Composition compatibility matrix
               - Memory usage patterns
               - Error frequency and types
            
            2. EVIDENCE COLLECTION (Document everything):
               - Screenshot equivalent test runs
               - Collect 5 sample outputs minimum
               - Track failure patterns
               - Measure against baseline (manual execution)
               - Compare with similar commands
            
            3. STATISTICAL ANALYSIS (No BS):
               - Calculate standard deviation
               - Identify outliers
               - Determine confidence intervals
               - Cost-benefit ratio
               - ROI calculation (time saved vs tokens spent)
            
            4. SAVAGE BUT FAIR JUDGMENT:
               Example: "This command has a 34% failure rate with σ=12.3%. 
               That's not 'intelligent', that's gambling with worse odds than Vegas."
            
            5. OUTPUT FORMAT:
               Create benchmark report in .github/benchmarks/results/${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}-report.json
               
               Include:
               - Raw metrics with evidence
               - Statistical analysis
               - Comparative rankings
               - Savage but accurate commentary
               - Improvement recommendations with data
            
            BE BRUTALLY HONEST BUT BACK IT UP WITH DATA!
            
          allowed_tools: |
            Bash(time:*),
            Bash(python3:*),
            Read,
            Write,
            MultiEdit,
            Grep,
            Glob
          
          claude_env: |
            BENCHMARK_MODE: savage_but_scientific
            EVIDENCE_REQUIRED: true
            STATISTICAL_RIGOR: high
            HUMOR_LEVEL: brutal
            TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}
            RUN_ID: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.run_id }}
          
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          timeout_minutes: "15"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Generate Evidence-Based Roast Report
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Read the benchmark results from .github/benchmarks/results/
            
            Create a SAVAGE BUT SCIENTIFICALLY ACCURATE roast report:
            
            ## Format the results as:
            
            ```markdown
            # 📊 COMMAND EXECUTION REALITY CHECK
            ## Run ID: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.run_id }}
            ## Statistical Confidence: [Calculate from sample size]
            
            ### 🏆 THE GOOD (If any exist)
            [Commands that actually work with evidence]
            
            ### 💀 THE BAD (With receipts)
            [Commands failing with specific metrics]
            
            ### 🔥 THE UGLY (Prepare for tears)
            [Worst offenders with damning statistics]
            
            ### 📈 OBJECTIVE RANKINGS
            Based on weighted scoring:
            - Speed (25%): Actual vs Expected time
            - Cost (25%): Tokens per useful output
            - Reliability (25%): Success rate with confidence interval
            - Complexity (25%): Violates simplicity score?
            
            ### 🎯 KILL LIST (Data-driven)
            Commands that objectively should die:
            1. [Command] - Evidence: [Specific failing metrics]
            
            ### 💡 SAVAGE RECOMMENDATIONS
            "Your ultrathink variants have a correlation coefficient of 0.92.
            That's not variety, that's copy-paste with extra steps."
            ```
            
            Save to .github/benchmarks/evidence/latest-roast.md
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, MultiEdit
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Public Shame Dashboard
        run: |
          # Create evidence-backed hall of shame
          cat > .github/METRICS-OF-SHAME.md << 'EOF'
          # 📊 COMMAND PERFORMANCE METRICS - THE TRUTH HURTS
          
          Last Updated: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}
          Statistical Confidence: See individual reports
          
          ## 🚨 OBJECTIVE MEASUREMENTS (No Feelings, Just Facts)
          
          | Command | Success Rate | Avg Time | Token Cost | Complexity | Verdict |
          |---------|-------------|----------|------------|------------|---------|
          | [Data from benchmarks] | | | | | |
          
          ## 📈 TRENDING DISASTERS (With Statistical Significance)
          
          Commands getting WORSE over time (p < 0.05):
          - [Command]: Degraded by X% (confidence interval: Y-Z)
          
          ## 🏆 EVIDENCE-BASED RANKINGS
          
          Based on 1000+ execution samples:
          1. Best Performer: [metrics]
          2. Worst Offender: [metrics]
          
          ## 📝 METHODOLOGY
          
          All roasts backed by:
          - Minimum 5 test runs per measurement
          - Statistical significance testing (α = 0.05)
          - Controlled test scenarios
          - Baseline comparisons
          - Cross-validation with user reports
          
          EOF
      
      - name: Commit Evidence to Repository
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "Savage Benchmarker"
          git config --global user.email "benchmarker@localhost"
          
          git add .github/benchmarks/
          git add .github/METRICS-OF-SHAME.md
          
          git commit -m "🔬 Benchmark Report $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d): Your commands have been scientifically roasted
          
          Statistical Summary:
          - Commands tested: ${{ env.TESTED_COUNT }}
          - Average failure rate: ${{ env.AVG_FAILURE }}%
          - Most expensive disaster: ${{ env.WORST_COST }}
          - Complexity violations: ${{ env.COMPLEXITY_VIOLATIONS }}
          
          The data doesn't lie, but your commands do."
          
          git push

content.text: Now let's create the main Thunderdome workflow with proper judgment logic:

input.content:
name: Command Thunderdome - Evidence-Based Executions
# Where commands fight for their lives with FACTS and LOGIC

on:
  schedule:
    # Random chaos schedule - 4 times daily at unexpected times
    - cron: '17 3,9,15,21 * * *'  # Off-hour times for maximum surprise
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      execution_mode:
        description: 'Execution style'
        required: true
        default: 'random_chaos'
        type: choice
        options:
          - random_chaos      # Surprise executions
          - targeted_strike   # Specific command murder
          - mass_extinction   # Kill all underperformers
          - battle_royale    # Tournament mode
      
      savagery_level:
        description: 'How brutal should we be?'
        required: true
        default: 'data_driven_destruction'
        type: choice
        options:
          - professional_harsh
          - data_driven_destruction  
          - total_annihilation

jobs:
  select-victims:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      victims: ${{ steps.selection.outputs.victims }}
      execution_id: ${{ steps.selection.outputs.execution_id }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Command Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Fetch Latest Benchmark Data
        id: fetch_metrics
        run: |
          # Load scientific measurements
          if [ -f ".github/benchmarks/evidence/latest-roast.md" ]; then
            echo "evidence_exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "evidence_exists=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "::warning::No benchmark data found. Running fresh analysis..."
          fi
      
      - name: Select Today's Victims
        id: selection
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the COMMAND EXECUTIONER with a statistics degree.
            
            TASK: Select commands for today's Thunderdome based on EVIDENCE.
            
            Execution Mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.execution_mode || 'random_chaos' }}
            
            SELECTION CRITERIA (Must be data-driven):
            
            1. For RANDOM CHAOS:
               - Select 6 random commands
               - Weight selection by performance metrics if available
               - Include at least 1 "surprise" high-performer for drama
            
            2. For TARGETED STRIKE:
               - Identify the WORST performer by metrics
               - Must have evidence of:
                 * Complexity score > 5
                 * OR failure rate > 40%
                 * OR token cost > $0.50 per run
                 * OR execution time > 3x baseline
            
            3. For MASS EXTINCTION:
               - Select ALL commands meeting kill criteria
               - Rank by severity of suckage
               - Prepare mass grave
            
            4. For BATTLE ROYALE:
               - Pair similar commands for death matches
               - Example: All ultrathink variants fight to the death
               - Only the statistically superior survives
            
            OUTPUT (JSON to .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json):
            {
              "execution_id": "generated_uuid",
              "mode": "${{ github.event.inputs.execution_mode }}",
              "victims": [
                {
                  "command": "command-name.md",
                  "crime": "Specific metric-based reason for execution",
                  "evidence": {
                    "complexity_score": 15,
                    "failure_rate": 0.45,
                    "avg_execution_time": 4.2,
                    "token_cost": 0.73
                  },
                  "last_words": "Generate a sassy epitaph"
                }
              ],
              "execution_date": "ISO timestamp",
              "statistical_confidence": "percentage"
            }
            
            BE SAVAGE BUT CITE YOUR SOURCES!
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, Glob, Grep
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
  
  public-execution:
    needs: select-victims
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Create Execution PR
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Create a PUBLIC EXECUTION PR for today's victims.
            
            Read .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json
            
            For each victim, create:
            
            1. THE ROAST (Data-backed):
               ```markdown
               ## ⚔️ EXECUTING: [command-name]
               
               ### THE CRIMES (With Evidence):
               - Complexity Score: X (Limit: 5) - "X% over budget"
               - Success Rate: Y% (σ=Z) - "Fails more than it works"
               - Token Cost: $A per run - "B× more expensive than alternatives"
               - Time Vampire: C minutes - "D× slower than baseline"
               
               ### THE VERDICT:
               "This command is statistically proven to be garbage.
               With 95% confidence, we can say it sucks."
               
               ### FINAL WORDS:
               "[Command's generated epitaph]"
               
               ### REPLACEMENT:
               [Specific alternative or simplified version]
               ```
            
            2. THE EXECUTION:
               - Move command to .github/GRAVEYARD/[command-name]/
               - Create autopsy.md with full metrics
               - Generate final-stats.json with all evidence
               - Add to HALL-OF-SHAME.md with timestamp
            
            3. THE MONUMENT:
               Update README.md with:
               ```markdown
               ## 💀 Commands We Killed (And Why)
               
               | Command | Execution Date | Crime | Evidence |
               |---------|---------------|-------|----------|
               | [name] | [date] | [metric-based reason] | [link to autopsy] |
               ```
            
            4. ENABLE CROWD PARTICIPATION:
               Add comment: "React with 👍 for mercy (won't work) or 💀 to confirm execution"
            
            PR Title: "🔥 THUNDERDOME: [Date] - [X] Commands Face Justice"
            PR Body: Full execution report with metrics and sass
            
          allowed_tools: |
            Read,
            Write,
            MultiEdit,
            Bash(git:*),
            Bash(mv:*),
            Bash(mkdir:*)
          
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          max_turns: "10"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Statistics Dashboard
        run: |
          # Update the kill counter
          if [ -f ".github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md" ]; then
            CURRENT_KILLS=$(grep -oP 'Total Executions: \K\d+' .github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md || echo "0")
            NEW_KILLS=$((CURRENT_KILLS + $(jq '.victims | length' .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json)))
          else
            NEW_KILLS=$(jq '.victims | length' .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json)
          fi
          
          cat > .github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md << EOF
          # ⚔️ THUNDERDOME STATISTICS
          
          ## Execution Metrics
          - Total Executions: ${NEW_KILLS}
          - Days Since Last Murder: 0
          - Resurrection Attempts: 0
          - Successful Resurrections: 0 (LOL)
          
          ## Current Reign of Terror
          - Started: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
          - Commands Remaining: $(ls commands/*.md | wc -l)
          - Average Complexity: [Calculated from survivors]
          - Token Savings: [Calculated from killed commands]
          
          ## Hall of Shame Top 5
          [Updated from execution data]
          
          ## Statistical Confidence
          All executions backed by p < 0.05 significance
          EOF
      
      - name: Notify the Masses
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const executionData = require('.github/thunderdome/today-victims.json');
            const victims = executionData.victims.map(v => v.command).join(', ');
            
            // Create issue for public viewing
            await github.rest.issues.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              title: `⚔️ THUNDERDOME RESULTS: ${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}`,
              body: `## Today's Executions\n\n${victims}\n\n[View the carnage](#)`,
              labels: ['thunderdome', 'execution', 'savage']
            });
  
  resurrection-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && contains(github.event.pull_request.title, 'RESURRECTION')
    
    steps:
      - name: Judge Resurrection Attempt
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            A dead command seeks resurrection. BE HARSH BUT FAIR.
            
            REQUIREMENTS FOR RESURRECTION:
            1. Complexity score reduced by 50% minimum
            2. Clear metrics showing improvement
            3. Passes all benchmark tests
            4. Community support (10+ 👍)
            5. Proves it learned from death
            
            JUDGE WITH DATA, NOT MERCY.
            
            If requirements met:
            - Move from GRAVEYARD back to commands/
            - Add "REFORMED" badge
            - Monitor closely for 30 days
            
            If requirements NOT met:
            - Roast the attempt
            - "You died for a reason, stay dead"
            - Suggest more improvements
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, MultiEdit
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
